intro: Friday's story is Microsoft turning on its own foundational model stack — MAI-Transcribe-1, MAI-Voice-1, and MAI-Image-2 — signalling the quiet end of pure OpenAI dependency inside Microsoft's AI products. Meta confirmed 2026 AI capex of $115–135B (roughly double last year), Stanford's AI Index said China has 'nearly erased' the US benchmark lead, and DeepSeek V4 on Huawei Ascend 950PR chips is days from launch.

top_stories.headline: Microsoft AI Ships Three In-House Foundational Models — MAI-Transcribe-1, MAI-Voice-1, MAI-Image-2
top_stories.summary: Microsoft AI released three homegrown foundational models this week: MAI-Transcribe-1 for speech-to-text, MAI-Voice-1 for voice generation, and MAI-Image-2 for image generation. This is Microsoft's most explicit signal yet that it is building a parallel first-party AI stack alongside its OpenAI partnership — covering text, voice, and image generation across Copilot surfaces without having to route every call through OpenAI.

top_stories.headline: Stanford AI Index 2026: China Has 'Nearly Erased' the US AI Lead
top_stories.summary: Fortune's April 16 coverage of Stanford HAI's 2026 AI Index confirms China has nearly closed the benchmark gap with the US, as the flow of Chinese tech experts to America has slowed. Stanford's full report documents that China's top models now trail US frontier models by a single-digit percentage on most major benchmarks — a gap that was double digits just 14 months ago.

top_stories.headline: Meta Confirms 2026 AI Capex of $115–135B — Nearly Double 2025
top_stories.summary: Meta disclosed 2026 AI capital expenditures of $115–135 billion, almost twice the prior year's outlay. The spending funds training infrastructure for Muse Spark's successor and expanded inference capacity for 3.5B users across Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp. Combined with Microsoft's comparable capex and OpenAI's fresh $122B funding round, 2026 is on track to be the largest single-year AI infrastructure buildout in history.

top_stories.headline: DeepSeek V4 Launches on Huawei Ascend 950PR — Nvidia CEO Calls It a 'Formidable Threat'
top_stories.summary: DeepSeek V4 — a 1T-parameter, 37B-active MoE model — is launching this month running entirely on Huawei Ascend 950PR chips, bypassing Nvidia and AMD. DeepSeek withheld early access from US chipmakers to give Huawei more software-tuning time. Alibaba, ByteDance, and Tencent have already placed orders for hundreds of thousands of Huawei chips. Jensen Huang publicly warned that a fully optimised DeepSeek-Huawei stack would be a 'formidable threat' to US AI dominance.

top_stories.headline: ADP Ships Payroll Variance AI Agent to 40+ Countries as Enterprise Agents Cross 79% Adoption
top_stories.summary: ADP rolled out its Payroll Variance agent to enterprise clients in 40+ countries, making it one of the first agentic AI products running in global mission-critical finance workflows at scale. A parallel survey of 1,900 IT leaders found 96% of enterprises are now running AI agents in some form, and 79% have formal production deployments — agentic AI has officially moved from pilot to line-of-business infrastructure.

quick_hits.text: OpenAI added a new Codex-only seat with flexible pricing inside ChatGPT Enterprise, separating Codex access from full ChatGPT seats for the first time — a clear signal that Codex is being priced as a standalone developer product.

Microsoft cut the minimum Microsoft 365 Copilot threshold for its 40% enterprise discount from 1,500 to 1,000 licenses, opening the reduced pricing tier to mid-market customers and accelerating Copilot penetration outside the Fortune 500.
Microsoft shipped Agent Framework 1.0 this week with stable APIs, native Model Context Protocol support, and a browser-based DevUI that visualises agent execution and tool calls in real time.

Cloudflare and GoDaddy announced a joint framework letting website owners control exactly how AI agents can access and use their content — an early attempt to build programmable consent into the agentic web.

Luma AI launched Innovative Dreams, a full AI-native production company formed with Wonder Project — the first serious attempt to vertically integrate an AI video model into a studio-grade production pipeline.

india_roundup.text: TCS closed FY26 (ending March 31) with $2.3B in annualised AI revenue, up from $1.8B in Q3 — a 28% sequential-quarter jump that makes TCS one of the largest AI-services revenue lines of any Indian IT firm.

NASSCOM's AI Code Sarathi Engineer-phase agentic AI hackathon deadline extended to today, April 17 — part of India's push to build the world's largest pipeline of operational AI engineers via public-private skilling.

Sarvam AI closed its $350M raise at a $1.5B valuation with Lightspeed, Khosla, Nvidia, and Amazon participating — alongside ₹246.72 crore of public compute + funding support under the IndiaAI Mission.

India's IndiaAI Compute Portal will add more than 20,000 GPUs to its existing base of 38,000 — a 50%+ capacity expansion announced at the India AI Impact Summit 2026, targeting sovereign-model training workloads.

Infosys unveiled its AI First Value Framework, claiming it is uniquely positioned to capture an AI services opportunity it sizes at $300B+ — formalising the Topaz + Cobalt stack as Infosys's flagship enterprise AI offering ahead of Q4 FY26 results on April 23.

closing_insight: The quiet headline of the week is not a new model or a new round — it is Microsoft shipping three in-house foundational models while OpenAI takes $50B from Amazon. Both sides of the most important partnership in AI are now building the optionality to walk away.
